step.title: Align with User
step.goal: Present the Writing Plan to the user. Lighter checkpoint than D or S phases (P is tactical), but still get explicit confirmation before ticket-write begins.

substep.title: Present Plan
substep.ask:
Writing plan proposal:
{{plan_draft}}
Approve to proceed to ticket-write?
- Confirm (Recommended if it looks right): lock the plan and proceed - Adjust writing order: change section sequence - Adjust subagent plan: change which sections dispatch subagents - Adjust checkpoints: add, remove, or move review pauses - Adjust length estimates: change my expectations on section length - Loop back to ticket-structure: this exposes a structural issue

check.action: Apply the adjustment. Re-loop to substep 3a. Iteration cap of 3 (P is tactical; deeper churn signals a structural issue better solved by loop-back).

Note in questions file: "Plan-phase loop-back: structural issue surfaced — {{specifics}}." Stamp last_updated. Exit cleanly. User invokes ticket-structure via menu (TS).
As with ticket-structure→ticket-design loop-back, this does NOT increment ticket-design's strike counter — tactical loop-back is not a foundational reversal.

section.title: P is tactical, not foundational
section.description: Adjustments at this phase are normal and cheap. The plan can change without invalidating the Design Summary or the Structure Outline. Strike counter does not apply here — even big reorderings are still tactical.

section.title: When to loop back vs adjust

rules.rule: Adjust: the section list is right, but the order or dispatch plan is off
Adjust: a length estimate is wildly wrong
Loop back to ticket-structure: a section in the outline doesn't actually need to exist (or one's missing)
Loop back to ticket-design: a section's existence reveals a design gap (rare; usually surfaces in ticket-structure)
